title: Abandoning the Prayer
subtitle: The First Deed Abandoned by Those Who Inherit Loss

verses.arabic: فَخَلَفَ مِن بَعْدِهِمْ خَلْفٌ أَضَاعُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَاتَّبَعُوا الشَّهَوَاتِ ۖ فَسَوْفَ يَلْقَوْنَ غَيًّا
verses.translation: But there came after them successors who neglected prayer and pursued desires; so they are going to meet evil —
They will say, 'We were not of those who prayed.'

verses.arabic: فَوَيْلٌ لِّلْمُصَلِّينَ ﴿٤﴾ الَّذِينَ هُمْ عَن صَلَاتِهِمْ سَاهُونَ
verses.translation: So woe to those who pray — [but] who are heedless of their prayer.

brief_explanation: The Quran identifies abandoning prayer as a defining characteristic of those who will enter Hellfire. When the people of Hell are asked what led them to Saqar, their very first answer is 'We were not of those who prayed' (74:43), establishing salah as the foundational act that separates the believer from the condemned. The Quran describes those who neglect prayer and chase worldly desires as meeting 'ghayy' — a valley of extreme punishment in Hell. The Prophet (peace be upon him) further confirmed that the covenant between a person and Islam is salah, and whoever abandons it has broken that covenant.
capital_punishment: Scholars differ on the ruling for the one who abandons salah entirely out of laziness (without denying its obligation). The Hanbali school holds that such a person is to be executed as an apostate if they refuse to pray after being ordered by the ruler. The Maliki and Shafi'i schools rule that they are executed as a hadd (not apostasy) after being given time to pray and refusing. The Hanafi school holds that they are imprisoned and beaten until they pray but not executed. One who abandons salah while denying its obligation is unanimously considered an apostate and is subject to the ruling of apostasy.
